crossref.abstract: <jats:title>Abstract</jats:title><jats:p>Experimental characterization of the transition state poses a significant challenge due to its fleeting nature. Negative ion photodetachment offers a unique tool for probing transition states and their vicinity. However, this approach is usually limited to Franck-Condon regions. For example, high-lying Feshbach resonances with an excited HF stretching mode (<jats:italic>v</jats:italic><jats:sub>HF</jats:sub> = 2-4) were recently identified in the transition-state region of the F + NH<jats:sub>3</jats:sub> → HF + NH<jats:sub>2</jats:sub> reaction through photo-detaching FNH<jats:sub>3</jats:sub><jats:sup>−</jats:sup> anions, but the direct photodetachment failed to observe the lower-lying <jats:italic>v</jats:italic><jats:sub>HF</jats:sub> = 0,1 resonances and bound states due apparently to negligible Franck-Condon factors. Indeed, these weak transitions can be resonantly enhanced via a dipole-bound state (DBS) formed between an electron and the polar FNH<jats:sub>3</jats:sub> species. In this study, we unveil a series of Feshbach resonances and bound states along the F + NH<jats:sub>3</jats:sub> reaction path via a DBS by combining high-resolution photoelectron spectroscopy with high-level quantum dynamical computations. This study presents an approach for probing the activated complex in a reaction by negative ion photodetachment through a DBS.</jats:p>
title: Probing the activated complex of the F + NH3 reaction via a dipole-bound state
